jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase after impact
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase in opening - high character integration but routine execution

jokes.text: [Whoopee Cushion Farts] - [Whimpers] - [Laughs]
jokes.punchline: Homer falls for obvious prank then laughs
jokes.explanation: Basic whoopee cushion gag - relies on cheap laughs

jokes.text: Whoa, a badger! Sorry, man. You can't crash here.
jokes.punchline: Homer treats badger like unwelcome houseguest
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Homer's casual tone toward dangerous situation

jokes.text: Then what's this? - That says 'corn,' Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart misreading can label looking for stoats
jokes.punchline: Complete misreading reveals Bart's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Bart's reading incompetence

jokes.text: Can we borrow your woodpecker? - I guess so. But we need him back by 6:00. It's his birthday.
jokes.punchline: Todd's innocent concern about woodpecker's birthday
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect Flanders family characterization - treating pet woodpecker birthday seriously

jokes.text: Badger, my ass. It's probably Milhouse.
jokes.punchline: Assumes it's just Milhouse hiding
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's dismissiveness

jokes.text: It's a badger, all right, or possibly a griffin.
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests mythological creature as alternative
jokes.explanation: Good Homer absurdism - treating griffin as plausible alternative

jokes.text: How did the badger do that without ripping your shirt? - What am I, a tailor?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking logical question about cartoon physics
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about cartoon logic with good Homer response

jokes.text: 939? What the hell is that? Oh, my life is ruined!
jokes.punchline: Immediate dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overreaction to minor inconvenience

jokes.text: The six is closer to the three, so you got convenience there. But the nine has less to do with Satan, which is a plus in this religious world of ours.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre Satan reference as legitimate consideration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating Satan connection as practical concern

jokes.text: Don't forget the leaflets they dropped from the space shuttle. And the two weeks we all spent at area code camp.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming no warning about area code change
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd examples of extensive notification
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdism - space shuttle leaflets and area code camp are wonderfully ridiculous

jokes.text: Joan Collins must be in town. - Joan Collins? That girl sleeps with everybody!
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent misunderstanding of reputation
jokes.explanation: Decent Homer naivety humor about celebrity gossip

jokes.text: I've been a fan of the Who since the very beginning when they were the Hillbilly Bugger Boys!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming longtime fandom of The Who
jokes.punchline: Completely fictional early band name
jokes.explanation: Good Homer fake expertise with absurd made-up band name

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Phoney McRingring, mascot and president of the telephone company.
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake, on-the-nose name
jokes.explanation: Transparent corporate satire with obvious fake name

jokes.text: It's a movie, Dad. - Quiet, honey. Daddy's asking the man a question.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to interact with educational film
jokes.punchline: Homer's earnest belief he can communicate with screen
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - believing he can talk to movie characters

jokes.text: Are you stupider than a monkey? - How big of a monkey?
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it literally and asks for specifications
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer literal-mindedness - perfect economy and character integration

jokes.text: I accuse the phone company of making that film on purpose! - Well, of course we did.
jokes.punchline: Company rep's matter-of-fact confirmation of obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's conspiracy theory with corporate honesty

jokes.text: Homer's right! We're gettin' the Joan Collins special!
jokes.punchline: References earlier Joan Collins mattress joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Joan Collins joke with Moe's characteristic crudeness

jokes.text: Fellow 939'ers, I say we break off and form our own city!
jokes.setup: Homer leading revolt against area code discrimination
jokes.punchline: Extreme solution to minor phone inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Good Homer escalation - creating city over phone numbers is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Now we just sit back and wait for an N.F.L. Franchise.
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive expectation of immediate NFL team
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American city priorities with Homer's sports obsession

jokes.text: I represent the Arizona Cardinals. - Keep walking.
jokes.punchline: Homer rejecting worst NFL franchise
jokes.explanation: Perfect sports humor timing and economy - Cardinals as bottom-tier franchise

jokes.text: Say, I couldn't help but overhear. I represent the Arizona Cardinals. - Keep walking.
jokes.setup: Someone offers Homer exactly what he just wished for
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately rejects the Cardinals as not worth having
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectation - Homer gets what he wants but it's not what he really wanted. Good NFL humor about bad franchises.

jokes.text: Good decision there, Homer. You showed a lot of poise.
jokes.setup: Homer rudely dismissed the Cardinals representative
jokes.punchline: Moe describes Homer's rude behavior as showing 'poise'
jokes.explanation: Mild ironic humor from Moe complimenting Homer's rudeness. Not particularly strong but adds to the moment.

jokes.text: I reluctantly accept this highly-paid, glamorous job.
jokes.punchline: Fake reluctance while describing obvious benefits
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent self-interest disguised as sacrifice

jokes.text: We have a better town bird. - Oh, yeah? What is it? - The bluebird. - Damn it.
jokes.punchline: Quimby's frustrated defeat over bird superiority
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of petty civic rivalry with Quimby's defeat

jokes.text: Sweetie, you know your mother and I only stay together for the sake of my political career.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests parents shouldn't split up over every fight
jokes.punchline: Homer reverses the usual 'staying together for the kids' trope
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that subverts a common saying. Shows Homer's self-centered worldview perfectly while being genuinely clever.

jokes.text: Sweetie, you know your mother and I only stay together for the sake of my political career.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting parents shouldn't split over disagreements
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting cynical reason for marriage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reversing typical 'staying together for the kids' trope

jokes.text: Give it back! That's my novelty flying disk. / You're in Olde Springfield now!
jokes.punchline: Territorial dispute over park boundaries
jokes.explanation: Shows how petty the town division has become through children's conflict

jokes.text: Hey, his pants are in our park too! - Get him!
jokes.punchline: Extending territorial claim to person's clothing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of territorial logic

jokes.text: Oh, no worries there. That's Henderson's toilet paper!
jokes.setup: Marge wanting quality for expensive toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Specific brand name for toilet paper as premium product
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdism with branded toilet paper as luxury item

jokes.text: Hey, the TV man is talking about us.
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike excitement about TV attention
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive excitement about negative media coverage

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? They think they're better than us, huh? Bart, c'mere a minute! - You c'mere a minute! - Oh, yeah?
jokes.punchline: Immediately using exact phrases mentioned as lowbrow
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate callback to news report - perfect timing and irony

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? They think they're better than us, huh? Bart, c'mere a minute! - You c'mere a minute! - Oh, yeah?
jokes.setup: TV report claims New Springfield residents use lowbrow expressions
jokes.punchline: Homer and Bart immediately use exactly those expressions
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic humor - characters immediately prove the stereotype right. Shows their lack of self-awareness perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You can't do heart surgery in the dark! - Sounds like a wager to me. - I'll take a piece of that.
jokes.punchline: Treating life-threatening surgery as betting opportunity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Springfield's gambling addiction even during medical crisis

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! Sweet glayvin! Oh, good God! Hey, wait, wait, wait! Ow! Oh! Oh! Sir Isaac's legs are hurting!
jokes.punchline: Frink's panicked gibberish and third-person reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink character comedy with his signature exclamations

jokes.text: Those rich, snobby Indians.
jokes.setup: Beer dumping incident reminiscent of Boston Tea Party
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused historical reference
jokes.explanation: Homer's historical ignorance mixing up Tea Party participants

jokes.text: There's nothing like revenge for getting back at people. - I don't know. Vengeance is good.
jokes.punchline: Moe treating synonyms as different options
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with characters missing synonymous nature

jokes.text: There's nothing like revenge for getting back at people. - I don't know. Vengeance is good.
jokes.setup: Homer makes a tautological statement about revenge
jokes.punchline: Moe treats revenge and vengeance as different concepts
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing both characters' limited vocabulary and logical thinking. Homer's tautology is characteristically meaningless.

jokes.text: Because of you, we're all takin' golden showers!
jokes.punchline: Moe's innocent use of sexual slang term
jokes.explanation: Double entendre humor with Moe's innocent delivery

jokes.text: We should call the guys they used. / [Homer calling Berlin Wall builders gets wrong area code]
jokes.setup: Homer wants to build wall like Berlin
jokes.punchline: Can't call because of area code problem they're trying to solve
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular irony - area code prevents solving area code problem

jokes.text: And then a wave of disease should help to- Hey! Stop streaming over the wall!
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by people fleeing his insane leadership
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer's casual mention of disease as population control

jokes.text: They couldn't take one lousy famine.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive attitude toward starvation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer callousness - treating famine as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? - Duh! Trashing the hotel room.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of rock star cliché
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of rock star behavior

jokes.text: But we have a handshake agreement with a concert promoter... and that's a sacred bond. - [All] Sacred bond.
jokes.punchline: All members solemnly repeating 'sacred bond'
jokes.explanation: Gentle mockery of aging rockers taking business seriously

jokes.text: Keith Partridge? Who huddle!
jokes.setup: Homer's insulting comparison to teen pop star
jokes.punchline: The Who treating it like serious strategic consultation
jokes.explanation: Good satire of rock band dynamics and Keith Partridge insult effectiveness

jokes.text: I opened for the Who at Woodstock. I came out in a Beatle wig with a ukulele. Hendrix said he almost plotzed. His exact words.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly inappropriate performance and Yiddish from Hendrix
jokes.explanation: Excellent Grampa tall tale with perfect absurd details and Hendrix using Yiddish

jokes.text: That fat, dumb and bald guy sure plays some real hardball.
jokes.setup: The Who commenting on Homer's tactics
jokes.punchline: Brutal accurate description delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Direct insult humor with Pete's clinical assessment of Homer

jokes.text: Look, Lisa. Daddy's in the Who. Ooh!
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent pride in Homer's musical 'achievement'
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with Marge's maternal pride in inappropriate context

jokes.text: Oh, come on. It plays itself! # Pac-Man fever # # Doo-doo-doo-doo # # It's a-driving me crazy #
jokes.punchline: Homer demonstrates how 'easy' it is with terrible singing
jokes.explanation: Homer's musical incompetence while claiming simplicity

jokes.text: Well, I'll be chuggered. That's the sticky wicket? Why not just buy telephones with auto ring-up? Or as you Yanks call it, 'speed dial.'
jokes.setup: British rock star learning about area code conflict
jokes.punchline: Simple solution with condescending American translation
jokes.explanation: Good British character voice solving American problem with casual condescension

jokes.text: My experiment? You're the one who came up with this whole idea-
jokes.setup: Marge blaming Homer for town division
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant deflection of obvious responsibility
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer responsibility avoidance - characteristic but not exceptional
